Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Reintroduction of the alternate Lin5 allele into modern varieties can increase fruit sugar content, improving flavor/sweetness; marker-assisted selection using chr9:3477979 SNP; trade-off: higher sugar is associated with smaller fruit size
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Overexpression of the alternate Lin5 allele (Asn366) results in significantly higher fruit sugar content compared to the reference allele (Asp366) or control, validating Lin5 as the causative gene for the chromosome 9 sugar QTL
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of reference vs alternate enzyme forms in FLA 8059 background; sugar quantification in fruit
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Extracellular (cell wall) invertase; cleaves sucrose into glucose and fructose
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic OE lines: InvR (reference allele, n=6) and InvA (alternate allele, n=17) in FLA 8059 background
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Alternate allele (Asn at position 366); associated with higher sugar content
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Accessions grown in Florida; F2 population evaluated
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Alternate allele increases flux from sucrose to hexose sugars (glucose + fructose) in fruit
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GWAS; QTL (F2 population); previously identified gene
Genes.Genetic_Population: 398-member diversity panel (GWAS); F2 population from Maglia Rosa Cherry × FLA 8059 (197 individuals)
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The chromosome 11 locus (SSC11.1, chr11:51186147) also significantly associated with sugar content by GWAS is also within a domestication/improvement sweep. Combined allele effects of chr9 and chr11 loci show the reference/reference combination (found in 47/48 modern cultivars) has the lowest sugar content.
Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing of 398 accessions; GWAS with 2,014,488 SNPs (MAF>5%); genotyping by sequencing of F2 population
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Negative correlation between fruit sugar content and fruit weight; Lin5 locus is within domestication and improvement sweep on chromosome 9, indicating selection for larger fruit led to loss of high-sugar allele
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Significantly higher sugar (glucose + fructose) in alternate allele OE lines vs reference allele OE lines and control FLA 8059 (Fig. 1G)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lin5 encodes a cell wall invertase on chromosome 9 identified by both GWAS and F2 QTL mapping as controlling fruit sugar (glucose + fructose) content. 2. A causal SNP causes an Asn-to-Asp change at position 366; the alternate (Asn) allele confers higher sugar content when overexpressed transgenically. 3. The Lin5 locus resides within a domestication/improvement sweep; 47/48 modern cultivars carry the low-sugar reference allele, indicating inadvertent loss of the high-sugar allele during selection for larger fruit. 4. Strong negative correlation between fruit weight and sugar content across multiple QTL allele combinations in the F2 population.
Genes.Variety: FLA 8059 (control/background); Maglia Rosa Cherry × FLA 8059 F2 population

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Selection for the alternate allele at the E8 locus can reduce undesirable medicinal-tasting volatiles (guaiacol and methylsalicylate), improving consumer liking; marker-assisted selection using chr9:69296937
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Antisense E8 line shows reduced guaiacol and methylsalicylate levels, mimicking the pattern of accessions carrying the alternate allele; reference allele accessions have 4.8-fold more methylsalicylate and 5.5-fold more guaiacol than alternate allele accessions
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Antisense transgenic line; volatile quantification compared to control cultivar Ailsa Craig
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Ripening-specific gene; precise enzymatic mechanism unknown but modulates volatile synthesis, possibly through ethylene content regulation
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Antisense E8 line vs. control cultivar Ailsa Craig
Genes.Expression_Pattern: One of the most abundantly expressed ripening-specific genes in tomato fruit
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Alternate allele at chr9:69296937; associated with lower (desirable) guaiacol and methylsalicylate content
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Guaiacol, methylsalicylate (flavor volatiles negatively contributing to consumer liking)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Loss of E8 function reduces levels of guaiacol and methylsalicylate (undesirable 'medicinal' volatiles) and modulates levels of multiple other volatiles without abolishing their synthesis
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: chr9:69296937; three significantly associated SNPs in upstream intergenic region, one in intron, one in downstream intergenic region of E8
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Guaiacol and methylsalicylate are described as 'medicinal' volatiles proposed to negatively contribute to consumer liking. Methylsalicylate is derived from the stress-associated phytohormone salicylic acid. E8 is one of the most abundantly expressed ripening-specific genes in tomato fruit.
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Modulates levels of multiple volatiles beyond guaiacol and methylsalicylate
Genes.Pathway_Name: Volatile metabolism; guaiacol and methylsalicylate biosynthesis
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Reference allele: 4.8-fold more methylsalicylate and 5.5-fold more guaiacol than alternate allele
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: May increase ethylene content, which in turn could affect expression of genes involved in volatile synthesis
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E8 (Solyc09g089580) is identified by GWAS as the causative gene for a chromosome 9 locus affecting guaiacol and methylsalicylate content in ripe fruit. 2. Antisense E8 transgenic lines show reduced guaiacol and methylsalicylate, validating E8 as the causal gene. 3. Reference allele accessions have 4.8-fold and 5.5-fold higher methylsalicylate and guaiacol, respectively, than alternate allele accessions. 4. E8 modulates but does not abolish volatile synthesis; its mechanism may involve ethylene regulation.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative volatile content (normalized to control Ailsa Craig)

Title: A chemical genetic roadmap to improved tomato flavor
